source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure That Cloud KMS Cryptokeys Are Not Anonymously or Publicly Accessible

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure That Cloud KMS Cryptokeys Are Not Anonymously or Publicly Accessible
input_row.description: It is recommended that the IAM policy on Cloud KMS cryptokeys should restrict anonymous and/or public access.
input_row.rationale: Granting permissions to allUsers or allAuthenticatedUsers allows anyone to access the dataset. Such access might not be desirable if sensitive data is stored at the location. In this case, ensure that anonymous and/or public access to a Cloud KMS cryptokey is not allowed. Impact: Removing the binding for allUsers and allAuthenticatedUsers members denies accessing cryptokeys to anonymous or public users.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud CLI 1. List all Cloud KMS Cryptokeys. gcloud kms keys list --keyring=[key_ring_name] --location=global -- format=json | jq '.[].name' 2. Ensure the below command's output does not contain allUsers or allAuthenticatedUsers. gcloud kms keys get-iam-policy [key_name] --keyring=[key_ring_name] -- location=global --format=json | jq '.bindings[].members[]'
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud CLI 1. List all Cloud KMS Cryptokeys. gcloud kms keys list --keyring=[key_ring_name] --location=global -- format=json | jq '.[].name'  2. Remove IAM policy binding for a KMS key to remove access to allUsers and allAuthenticatedUsers using the below command. gcloud kms keys remove-iam-policy-binding [key_name] -- keyring=[key_ring_name] --location=global --member='allAuthenticatedUsers' -- role='[role]' gcloud kms keys remove-iam-policy-binding [key_name] -- keyring=[key_ring_name] --location=global --member='allUsers' --role='[role]' Default Value: By default Cloud KMS does not allow access to allUsers or allAuthenticatedUsers. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/kms/keys/remove-iam-policy- binding 2. https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/kms/keys/set-iam-policy 3. https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/kms/keys/get-iam-policy 4. https://cloud.google.com/kms/docs/object-hierarchy#key_resource_id  Additional Information: [key_ring_name] : Is the resource ID of the key ring, which is the fully-qualified Key ring name. This value is case-sensitive and in the form: projects/PROJECT_ID/locations/LOCATION/keyRings/KEY_RING You can retrieve the key ring resource ID using the Cloud Console: 1. Open the Cryptographic Keys page in the Cloud Console. 2. For the key ring whose resource ID you are retrieving, click the More icon (3 vertical dots). 3. Click Copy Resource ID. The resource ID for the key ring is copied to your clipboard. [key_name] : Is the resource ID of the key, which is the fully-qualified CryptoKey name. This value is case-sensitive and in the form: projects/PROJECT_ID/locations/LOCATION/keyRings/KEY_RING/cryptoKeys/KEY You can retrieve the key resource ID using the Cloud Console: 1. Open the Cryptographic Keys page in the Cloud Console. 2. Click the name of the key ring that contains the key. 3. For the key whose resource ID you are retrieving, click the More icon (3 vertical dots). 4. Click Copy Resource ID. The resource ID for the key is copied to your clipboard. [role] : The role to remove the member from.
input_row.impact: Removing the binding for allUsers and allAuthenticatedUsers members denies accessing cryptokeys to anonymous or public users.
input_row.references: 1. https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/kms/keys/remove-iam-policy- binding 2. https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/kms/keys/set-iam-policy 3. https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/kms/keys/get-iam-policy 4. https://cloud.google.com/kms/docs/object-hierarchy#key_resource_id  Additional Information: [key_ring_name] : Is the resource ID of the key ring, which is the fully-qualified Key ring name. This value is case-sensitive and in the form: projects/PROJECT_ID/locations/LOCATION/keyRings/KEY_RING You can retrieve the key ring resource ID using the Cloud Console: 1. Open the Cryptographic Keys page in the Cloud Console. 2. For the key ring whose resource ID you are retrieving, click the More icon (3 vertical dots). 3. Click Copy Resource ID. The resource ID for the key ring is copied to your clipboard. [key_name] : Is the resource ID of the key, which is the fully-qualified CryptoKey name. This value is case-sensitive and in the form: projects/PROJECT_ID/locations/LOCATION/keyRings/KEY_RING/cryptoKeys/KEY You can retrieve the key resource ID using the Cloud Console: 1. Open the Cryptographic Keys page in the Cloud Console. 2. Click the name of the key ring that contains the key. 3. For the key whose resource ID you are retrieving, click the More icon (3 vertical dots). 4. Click Copy Resource ID. The resource ID for the key is copied to your clipboard. [role] : The role to remove the member from.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit process involves using specific GCP CLI commands (`gcloud`) to list and check IAM policies on Cloud KMS cryptokeys to ensure that they do not grant access to `allUsers` or `allAuthenticatedUsers`. 
- The availability of Google Cloud SDK methods and direct CLI commands to complete these steps suggests that the control can be fully verified via GCP APIs and CLI, making it suitable for automation.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_kms_cryptokey_public_access_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **GCP CLI Commands:** Use the `gcloud kms keys list` followed by `gcloud kms keys get-iam-policy` commands to check Cloud KMS cryptokeys.
- **Resource Enumeration:** List all cryptokeys in all key rings across all projects and locations.
- **Check Configuration Property:** Validate that no IAM policy binding includes members `allUsers` or `allAuthenticatedUsers`.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
  - **Pass:** The IAM policy of the cryptokey does not include any binding that grants permissions to `allUsers` or `allAuthenticatedUsers`.
  - **Fail:** If any IAM policy binding is found which includes `allUsers` or `allAuthenticatedUsers`.
- **API Calls:** Utilize Google Cloud SDK to automate these commands within a script or CSPM tool.
- **Expected Values:** Cryptokey IAM policies should only grant access to specific, authenticated users or groups, not public or anonymous users.

This ensures that cryptokeys remain inaccessible to unauthorized entities, maintaining the security of sensitive data encrypted with KMS.
step2_review:
---

### 1. APPROACH VALIDATION: **AGREE**

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:**  
  The classification as "Automated" is correct. This control can be fully audited using GCP APIs and CLI tools.
- **GCP API/SDK/CLI Feasibility:**  
  The required information (IAM policies on KMS cryptokeys) is accessible via the `gcloud` CLI and GCP REST APIs. Specifically, `gcloud kms keys list` and `gcloud kms keys get-iam-policy` are valid commands for enumerating keys and retrieving their IAM policies.
- **Other GCP Tools:**  
  While GCP Config (now called "Config Controller") does not natively support KMS key IAM policy checks as a managed rule, the approach via CLI/API is industry standard and aligns with best practices.

---

### 2. TECHNICAL ACCURACY: **Score: 8/10**

- **Automation Details:**  
  - Correctly identifies the need to enumerate all cryptokeys across all key rings, locations, and projects.
  - Properly uses `gcloud kms keys list` and `gcloud kms keys get-iam-policy`.
  - Correctly checks for `allUsers` and `allAuthenticatedUsers` in IAM policy bindings.
- **Gcloud Command Details:**  
  - **Minor Omission:** The command to list all cryptokeys should specify `--location` and `--keyring` (or use `--location=-` and `--keyring=-` to enumerate all).  
    Example:  
    ```sh
    gcloud kms keys list --location=LOCATION --keyring=KEYRING
    ```
    To automate across all, you need to loop through all locations and keyrings.
  - **IAM Policy Retrieval:**  
    ```sh
    gcloud kms keys get-iam-policy KEY_NAME --location=LOCATION --keyring=KEYRING
    ```
    This is correct, but the script must handle all combinations.
- **Program Name:**  
  - The name `gcp_kms_cryptokey_public_access_restricted` is descriptive, but could be more specific (see below).
- **Completeness:**  
  - The assessment does not mention handling multiple projects, which is critical in enterprise environments.
  - No mention of service accounts or custom roles, but these are not required for this specific check.

---

### 3. IMPROVEMENTS

- **Enumerate All Projects:**  
  - The script/automation should explicitly handle all GCP projects in the organization, not just the current project.
  - Suggest using `gcloud projects list` as part of the enumeration.
- **Enumerate All Locations and Keyrings:**  
  - Must loop through all locations (`gcloud kms locations list`) and all keyrings in each location.
- **Alternative API Methods:**  
  - The same can be achieved via REST API:  
    - `projects.locations.keyRings.cryptoKeys.list`
    - `projects.locations.keyRings.cryptoKeys.getIamPolicy`
  - For large orgs, using the API directly (with pagination) may be more scalable than CLI.
- **Program Name Suggestion:**  
  - For clarity and convention, consider:  
    `gcp_kms_cryptokey_iam_no_public_access`  
    or  
    `gcp_kms_cryptokey_iam_no_allusers`
- **Output/Reporting:**  
  - Recommend the script outputs the offending cryptokey resource names and their IAM bindings for audit traceability.
- **Edge Cases:**  
  - Consider if inherited IAM policies (from key rings or project level) could impact cryptokey access (though cryptokey IAM is not inherited from key ring, but project-level roles may grant access).
- **Performance:**  
  - For large orgs, ensure the script handles API rate limits and errors gracefully.

---

### 4. CONFIDENCE: **HIGH**

- The assessment is fundamentally correct and feasible with GCP APIs/CLI.
- Minor improvements are needed for completeness and scalability in large environments.

---

### 5. FINAL RECOMMENDATION: **Modify**

- **Keep as Automated**, but **modify** the automation details to:
  - Explicitly enumerate all projects, locations, and keyrings.
  - Provide example scripts or pseudocode for clarity.
  - Suggest a more precise program name (`gcp_kms_cryptokey_iam_no_public_access`).
  - Recommend outputting detailed findings for audit records.

---

**Summary:**  
The assessment is accurate and the control can be fully automated with GCP CLI/API. Some improvements are needed for completeness and clarity, especially for multi-project environments and naming conventions. The technical approach is sound and aligns with GCP security best practices.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions agree that this control can be fully audited using GCP APIs and CLI tools. The IAM policies for Cloud KMS cryptokeys are directly accessible via the Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud kms keys get-iam-policy`), and all necessary properties (IAM bindings, member types) are exposed for programmatic inspection. There is no need for manual inspection, as the process can be reliably automated to enumerate all cryptokeys and check for public/anonymous access.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
gcp_kms_cryptokey_public_access_restriction

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **GCP Resource/Service:** Cloud KMS Cryptokeys
- **API/CLI Method:**  
  - List all keyrings: `gcloud kms keyrings list --location=LOCATION`
  - List all cryptokeys: `gcloud kms keys list --location=LOCATION --keyring=KEYRING`
  - Get IAM policy for each cryptokey: `gcloud kms keys get-iam-policy [KEY_NAME] --location=LOCATION --keyring=KEYRING --format=json`
- **Properties to Check:**  
  - `.bindings[].members[]` in the IAM policy response for each cryptokey.
- **Pass Criteria:**  
  - No IAM policy binding for any cryptokey includes `allUsers` or `allAuthenticatedUsers` as a member.
- **Fail Criteria:**  
  - Any IAM policy binding for any cryptokey includes `allUsers` or `allAuthenticatedUsers` as a member.

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
There were no significant disagreements between the AI opinions. The second opinion provided minor technical clarifications (e.g., need to enumerate all locations and keyrings), which are incorporated in the automation details above.

**SOURCE:** CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
